events.title: Matcha Mahjong Mixer🍵
events.description:
Dear founders and investors,

If you made it this far, you're invited to the next Matcha Mahjong Mixer — brought to you by Leo AI and Reitler.

This is an evening for the people actually building it. The ones shipping in the middle of an industry that reshuffles itself with every new Claude and OpenAI release, who made the hard calls, carried the early risk, and know what it means to go from zero to something real.

We're creating a highly social environment where conversations and connections actually flow — far from the typical "future of work" panel. No decks, no pitches, no name-tag-and-business-card grind. Just a small room of sharp people and good conversation.

Here's the twist: we play mahjong. Four seats to a table, tiles in hand, matcha in your cup. It sounds unusual, and that's the point — nothing breaks the ice and gets strangers talking like sitting down to learn a game together. No experience needed. We'll teach you in ten minutes, and you'll be trash-talking your table by the second round.

The setting is intimate: a private members' club in Midtown, dim and unhurried, with a matcha courtesy of Reitler. Think founders across AI, SaaS, fintech, consumer, and everything in between — alongside the seed-to-Series-A investors who back them. People who've been in the trenches and have something worth saying.

Our goal is simple: we want you to leave with at least 3 high-value connections — the kind you'll actually use.

About the organizers

Leo AI uses social signals to help marketing teams launch new channels by turning hours of unused content into data-driven video storytelling. Finalist at the 2025 Startup Awards and one of the UK's top 10 AdTech startups. Leo AI empowers companies to focus on what matters most: telling their story to the audience most likely to become their customers.

Reitler Kailas & Rosenblatt LLP is a New York-based full service boutique law firm that delivers responsive, high quality legal services to sophisticated business clients. Reitler possesses leading capabilities in venture capital and private equity financing's, fund formation, mergers and acquisitions, securities offerings and capital markets, real estate and hospitality, as well as maritime and commercial litigation.

Resident Company Club is a curated workspace for startups, founders, and VCs, designed to foster meaningful connections and collaboration. Coworking promised community, but delivered strangers sharing Wi-Fi. Resident is a company club, where every member is intentionally selected for cultural fit and collective ambition. Learn more here.

Wanna join? Spots are limited and the guest list is curated to keep the founder-to-investor balance right. Secure yours below and make sure your perspective is part of the room.

We can't wait to see you there. 🍵🀄

events.title: Field Notes Session No. 5 in collaboration with Fabrik
events.description:
Every Field Note starts with one question: when do you feel most yourself?

The answer changes everything. People stop describing their jobs and start describing their actual lives.

Field Notes Sessions brings that same question into a room. We'll write, move, and sit with images — yours and mine. You'll look underneath a moment in your own life, name something you've been doing anyway, and read a stranger's story and find something true in it.

At the end, I'll share five patterns that have surfaced across fifty Field Notes — across every age, industry, and kind of life. Odds are good the room will have just lived every single one.

Please bring a photo with you of where you feel most yourself.

Come be part of the record.

P.S This Field Notes Session is in collaboration with Fabrik and will be hosted in the main space which means we will have more space! Please feel free to invite a pal, colleague or someone you think would enjoy this experience!

About Fabrik

Fabrik is a home for communities where everyone has a place to belong. With spaces designed to feel more like your living room than your office, Fabrik’s social spaces are vibrant hubs where people can come together in real life, explore interests, forge meaningful connections, and build a sense of community.

*By RSVPing, you agree to receive communications from Fabrik related to this gathering and future offerings. You may unsubscribe anytime.

Rooted in heritage and inspired by the idea that this coffee tradition deserves a more everyday place in the world, Subra is all about making people feel at home in it.

events.title: The Future of Biosecurity with SPC NYC
events.description:
Biosecurity work spans preventing, defending against, and recovering from biological threats to safeguard public health, ecosystems, and societal stability. It's gaining urgency as recent outbreaks strain detection and response and as AI lowers the barrier to engineered bio-threats.

Software is becoming central to the defense, and the technology industry is moving in with the formation of dozens of new startups and thesis-driven funds tackling biosecurity threats. We're bringing together leaders to show how these solutions get built and scaled. 

We'll discuss how software protects against outbreaks today, how AI reshapes the future threat landscape, how it can speed the deployment of monitoring systems and countermeasures, and what it would take to end all respiratory disease.

Speakers include: 

Spencer Weiss is the Technical Lead of Federal Health at Palantir. He's led projects that operate the US government's public health, bioscience research, and healthcare delivery. He's previously worked at startups, and is a graduate of the Jerome Fisher Program in Management and Technology at the University
of Pennsylvania.

Dr. Akhila Kosaraju is the CEO and co-founder of Phare Bio, a biotech social venture pioneering the use of generative AI to discover novel antibiotics in partnership with the Collins Lab at MIT. Phare launched with support from TED’s Audacious Project, has been awarded $27M from ARPA-H, and recently received support from Google.org’s Generative AI Accelerator. A physician and biotech executive, Dr. Kosaraju has led efforts across startups, government, and global health. She previously served as founding CEO of Variant Bio, an executive at SIGA Technologies, and a White House appointee in the Pentagon helping to oversee the Military Health System. She is a Member of the Council on Foreign Relations, co-founder of the Alliance to End Biological Threats, and a Lecturer at Stanford. She earned her M.D. from Columbia and her B.A. from Stanford.

Charlie Petty is the co-head of Intercept and a long time investor in global public health technologies. He previously held leadership and investing roles at GHIC, Adjuvant Capital and the Global Health Investment Fund. Prior public health investments include Curevo (Eli Lilly), Vaccine Company (Eli Lilly), Alydia Health (Merck), Iantech (Carl Zeiss), Minervax, Monod Bio, Eubiologics, Terrestrial Bio, Centivax, Excision Biosciences, Codagenix, and Vitrivax.

About the moderator: 

Amit Gupta is a member of SPC and the co-founder of Catalyze, an AI-powered platform for discovering, funding, and executing frontier science. Catalyze acts as a coordination layer across the research funding lifecycle, giving researchers visibility for their work, helping funders deploy capital with confidence, and reducing the friction between the two. The team works directly with institutions, foundations, and government agencies to build custom R&D infrastructure and AI tooling. Before Catalyze, Amit held multiple roles as a biotech operator and investor.

​About South Park Commons (SPC)

SPC is a technical community and venture fund dedicated to helping founders, researchers, and technologists figure out what to work on next—what we call the -1 to 0 stage of your career. We believe this is best accomplished in the most talent-dense community possible. If that's you, apply for SPC membership.

events.title: Touching Grass Tour 🌱 (Cadman Park Plaza)
events.description:
Touching Grass Tour 
We started Yoga Club as a way to move, unplug, and actually meet people. Join us for our summer series in partnership with the NYC Parks Dept!

☀️ Flowing vinyasa practice in the great outdoors
🌊 Style: All-levels flow (accessible + refreshing)
🙏 No yoga experience needed! Ideal for beginners and experienced yogis. We mean it!!

Please RSVP fast, we are limited in the number of spots we can accommodate.

📣 How to Sign Up 
Please RSVP for free here on Luma and present your ticket at the event.

BYOM (Bring Your Own Mat)

📍 Where & When 
Cadman Park Plaza (Look for the yellow Yoga Club flag!)

Gathering starts at 6:00pm. Practice begins once the space fills,  about 15–20 minutes after, and runs for an hour. Schmooze to follow.

🌿 What to Expect 
We're a club, not a class. Come as you are, say hello to the people around you, and settle in.

Our Vinyasa practices are for all levels and run about an hour. Expect to move and breathe through an energizing set of standing postures and rejuvenating poses on the ground.

We understand that life happens. Sometimes that means showing up late, in your PJs, or both. We leave some time at the beginning of our meetings to ease in and socialize.

After Yoga 
Stick around. Socializing afterwards.

📵 Your Phone
Touching Grass means stepping away from digital distractions.

We ask that you don't use your phone during the gathering. We don't take phones away. You can use yours for emergencies or step away from the group if you need to. We just ask everyone to follow the honor system.

It helps people slow down and stay present.

⭐ Our Instructor 
Dan Sohval came to yoga seeking ways to build inner discipline and mental balance. He is the founder of Yoga Club. A lifelong educator, he brings to his instruction the same humor and curiosity that guides his teaching in the classroom. He received his 200-hour certification at VeraYoga with additional training in yin and trauma-informed practices. Off the mat, he is a published author, teaches computer science, and is an avid fan of Korean food.

💫 About Yoga Club 
We bring people together through high quality, affordable yoga and community. We meet in creative spaces around the city to move, breathe, and meet new people.

events.title: Building Web-Powered AI Apps Without Code: Base44 & Tavily
events.description:
Join us at the Tavily HQ in NYC for a practical, hands-on workshop focused on building web applications using AI.

In this session, we will demonstrate how to use Base44 to generate functional web apps through text prompts, and how to connect Tavily to give those apps real-time web research capabilities.

The goal of this evening is to move past simply chatting with AI and learn how to build independent tools that solve real problems.

Who Is This For?

This workshop is designed for anyone curious about practical AI applications, regardless of technical background. It is a welcoming space for professionals in product management, sales, marketing, operations, or anyone looking to learn how to leverage these tools.

No prior coding experience is required. If you can navigate a web browser and write clear instructions, you can participate fully.

What You’ll Learn & Build

We will walk through the logic of app creation using natural language. You will see how Base44 structures the layout and database of an application, and how Tavily feeds clean, structured web data into it.

By the end of the two-hour session, you will have configured and deployed a simple, functional web tool—such as a personalized research assistant or a live market data aggregator.

Agenda

5:30 PM – 6:00 PM: Arrival and Networking

6:00 PM – 6:15 PM: Introduction to Base44 and Tavily

6:15 PM – 6:30 PM: Live Walkthrough (From prompt to live tool)

6:30 PM – 7:15 PM: Guided Build Session (Work on your own app with support from our team)

7:15 PM – 7:30 PM: Wrap-up and Casual Demos

Logistics & Requirements

Date: Thursday, July 9th

Time: 5:30 PM - 7:30 PM EST

Location: Tavily HQ, New York City (Exact address will be sent upon confirmation)

What to bring: Please bring a laptop and charger, along with a simple idea for a tool you would like to build.

events.title: WHOOPS - Interactive Art Pop-Up by ARTIACT
events.description:
We've been waiting to do this one! WHOOPS is ARTIACT's first Activation Pop-up, four days of art you can't just stand and look at.

Every work in the show is interactive, which means none of it finishes itself. It waits for you to touch it, move through it, or figure out how to talk to it. We brought six artists together because their work shares a refusal: it will not be looked at from a polite distance. So we built the show around what each piece needs, and we're asking you to do the same thing the work does, which is pay attention with your hands.

You'll see work by Lu Lyu, Han Bao, Miguel Gallego, Julia Daser, Minami Matsumoto, and Jackie Nam.

Four days, four nights, each one its own thing. Thursday opens the show. Friday through Sunday, we run daytime hours, 12 to 8 PM, with a different evening program every night, building to the loudest closing we can pull off.

Free to walk in and tune in for the evening programs.

Dress Code🫪: Anything that makes people go WHOOPS.

show up. touch something. stay.

Activated by ARTIACT
Activators: Alicia Zhang and Yasmine Wei

events.title: "I Wait" Music Video Premiere with Ali Sethi
events.description:
Premiere Screening of "I Wait" by Ali Sethi followed by a moderated discussion with artist Ali Sethi and director Haya Waseem, moderated by Puja Patel, former Editor in Chief of Pitchfork.

Doors open at 6:30pm. Screening begins promptly at 7pm.

Kindly note a confirmed RSVP does not guarantee admission. Seating is available on a first-come, first-served basis, and we encourage guests to arrive early.

---

Ali Sethi is a Pakistani-American singer, composer and storyteller whose music advances a syncretic South Asian identity. Fusing classical ragas with contemporary beats, his music feels “stealthily subversive” (The New Yorker) and marries the metaphors of Punjabi poetry with camp and queer sensibilities. In 2022, his song Pasoori became the first Pakistani single to top Spotify's Global Viral Chart, and was Google's most 'hum-to-search' song in the world. In 2022, he was named to Time's '100 Next' list, his music praised for showing that “differences of culture, language, religion and gender do not need to be antagonistic; they can, and always have, enriched us, and given us some of our greatest works of art.”

Haya Waseem is a Pakistani-born, Canadian filmmaker. Raised in Switzerland and based in Brooklyn, she is multilingual with a documentary editing background. Her debut feature, Quickening, premiered to critical acclaim at the Toronto International Film Festival. Her Work/Life campaign for Publicis Groupe won the Grand Prix for Good at Cannes Lions. Haya is an alumna of the Canadian Film Centre Director Lab and TIFF Filmmaker Lab. Haya brings her eclectic upbringing and craft driven approach, often shooting on 35mm and 65mm, to every project.

About Object & Animal
Object & Animal is a film production company spanning across music, advertising, documentary, and narrative. Our work has been recognized by the industry's leading awards,  including Cannes Lions, D&AD, British Arrows, Ciclope, and APA. In 2019, 2024, and 2025 we were named UKMVA Production Company of the Year.  We have produced work across genres for artists including Charli xcx, Billie Eilish, Harry Styles, Andre 3000, KATSEYE, Chappell Roan, Taylor Swift, Geese, and FKA Twigs, amassing billions of views. Our music videos have earned numerous MTV Video Music Awards and three Grammy nominations.

About NEW INC
Expanding on the New Museum’s commitment to new art and new ideas, NEW INC is the Museum’s cultural incubator supporting creative practitioners and small businesses working across art, design, and technology. Now in Year 12, NEW INC’s membership model continues to support a diverse range of creative practitioners with a values-driven program and safe space for gathering and developing new creative projects and businesses. In 2020, NEW INC launched ONX Studio, an XR accelerator for artists, in partnership with the Onassis Foundation. NEW INC was cofounded by New Museum’s Toby Devan Lewis Director Lisa Phillips and former Deputy Director Karen Wong in 2014 as the first museum-led cultural incubator.

events.title: Catalyst NYC: The Future of Translational Science
events.description:
Hosted by ResearchHub Foundation

Co-hosted by Nucleate and Cure

The systems and models that have long governed scientific research are being reimagined, creating space for the most consequential conversations science has seen in decades. Catalyst NYC will bring together a carefully selected group for substantive, high-signal discussions about what's working, how we adapt to a changing scientific landscape, and where the greatest opportunities lie.

The program will feature keynotes, panels, and networking, with focused dialogue on alternative funding models, open science infrastructure, and the emerging tools and organizations building a new ecosystem for scientific progress.

Who's in the room

Attendees span research science, venture and philanthropic funding, biotech, and scientific institution-building.

Agenda

9:30 – 10:00 AM · Registration & Coffee

10:00 – 10:05 AM · Welcome and Opening Remarks
Patrick Joyce

10:05 – 10:20 AM · The Cure Innovation Index
Seema Kumar

An overview of Cure's new innovation index.

10:20 – 11:05 AM · Panel: Discover, Develop, Deploy
Cliff Friedman · Dr. Kevin Tracey · Dr. Brian Kim

Improving the pipeline of discovery, development, and deployment.

11:05 – 11:15 AM · Coffee Break

11:15 – 12:00 PM · Keynote: Maximizing translational science through Innovation
Dr. Joni Rutter

A keynote on catalyzing translational science through innovation

12:00 – 1:30 PM · Lunch & networking

1:30 – 2:00 PM · An Alternative Model for Research Funding
Patrick Joyce · Dr. Jeff Koury

The ResearchHub funding and endowment model.

2:00 – 2:30 PM · NSF Technology, Innovation and Partnerships (TIP)
Dr. Bridget Turaga

2:30 – 2:45 PM · Coffee Break

2:45 – 3:00 PM · From Academia to Founder
Dr. Oliver Dodd

Transitioning early career researchers into founders.

3:00 – 3:30 PM · Going Founder Mode on Cancer
Jacob Stern

How AI can enable more personalized medicine.

3:30 – 3:45 PM · Closing Remarks

4:00 – 6:00 PM · Rooftop Cocktail Hour

Speakers

We are excited to share our confirmed speakers for Catalyst NYC.

Dr. Joni Rutter, Director of the National Center for Advancing Translational Science (NCATS) at the National Institute of Health (NIH), will deliver a keynote address.

Seema Kumar, CEO of Cure, brings deep experience at the intersection of science and industry, and will be joining us from Cure to speak on the Cure Innovation Index and what it reveals about the state of translational research today.

Dr. Kevin Tracey, President and CEO of the Feinstein Institutes for Medical Research and a pioneer in bioelectronic medicine, will join a panel on accelerating the pipeline from scientific discovery to clinical application.

Dr. Brian Kim, Professor and Vice Chair of Research at the Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai, will join a panel on accelerating the pipeline from scientific discovery to clinical application.

Cliff Friedman, Managing Director of Northwell Health, will join a panel on accelerating the pipeline from scientific discovery to clinical application.

Dr. Bridget Turaga, of the National Science Foundation, will speak on the NSF Directorate for Technology, Innovation and Partnerships (TIP).

Dr. Oliver Dodd, Co-founder of Nucleate, will speak to the challenge and opportunity of turning early-career researchers into the next generation of founders.

Jacob Stern, CEO of Team Sid and GP at Even One Ventures, will speak on how AI can enable more personalized medicine.

About

ResearchHub is an open-access platform co-founded by Brian Armstrong and Patrick Joyce to accelerate the pace of scientific research. The platform reimagines how science is funded, evaluated, and shared, featuring a peer review marketplace where proposals and preprints are reviewed openly and in real time, with reviewers compensated for their expertise via ResearchCoin (RSC). Through a preregistration-based funding model, anyone can directly fund rigorous, reproducible science, with all results published transparently.

events.title: NYC Pizza Crawl #16 - Long Island City
events.description:
Since the first Saturday of July falls on July 4th, i'm going to move the pizza crawl so everyone can enjoy the weekend.

Event Details

Date: Saturday, July 11, 2026.

Meeting Location: TBD

Meeting Time: 12:00PM

Length of crawl: About 3 hours.

Cost: Pay as you go. You are paying for your own pizza so you can eat as much as your heart desires!

Want to keep up with all our pizza adventures? Follow along on Instagram 👉 https://www.instagram.com/newyorkcitypizzacrawl/

What is ‘NYC Pizza Crawl?

We’re bringing people together from all backgrounds through a shared love of pizza. Each month, we explore a different NYC neighborhood, sampling slices from four standout pizza shops. But this isn’t just about the food—it’s about connection, conversation, and community.

What sets our crawls apart is the focus on building new friendships along the way. As you savor some of the city’s best slices, you’ll have plenty of opportunities to engage in great conversations and get to know your fellow pizza adventurers. After all, the heart of this group isn’t just pizza—it’s the people who come together to enjoy it.

Rules for Attendees

By attending a NYC Pizza Crawl event, you agree to the following:

Be Kind & Respectful to others

Let’s keep the good vibes going by being kind—to each other and to those around us. We’re here for great pizza and even better company, so let’s make sure everyone feels welcome and respected.

If you ever feel uncomfortable or notice someone acting inappropriately, please let me or a team member know right away. We have a zero-tolerance policy for bad behavior—anyone who violates this may be asked to leave and will not be welcome at future events. There is no three strike policy. One strike and you are out.

Kindness and respect are the foundation of this community. Let’s keep it that way.

Open to All

Whether you're a local NYC resident, a visitor from another state, or traveling from overseas, we warmly invite you to join our pizza crawls.

Social Atmosphere

Our events are naturally social and interactive. Please come prepared to engage with fellow pizza enthusiasts and enjoy lively conversations.

Allergy Awareness

If you have food allergies or gluten sensitivities, please exercise caution when eating. Many pizza restaurants may have cross-contamination in their kitchens, so stay vigilant.

Event RSVPs

To maintain the quality of our events, each one will have a limited number of spots. This ensures that everyone has a great experience without overcrowding and long wait times.

When you RSVP to an event, you’re committing to attend. We understand that sometimes plans change, so if you are unable to attend, please change your RSVP.

Your commitment helps us build a vibrant and active community. To be fair to others, repeated (2 strikes) no-shows in a row will result in being banned from future events.

Event Photos/Video

By participating in our events, you consent to being photographed or filmed. These photos and videos may be used for promotional and marketing purposes on Reddit and our website.

Disclaimer

Our events are independently organized and not affiliated with Reddit Inc. This group is volunteer-run with the goal of promoting the pizza community. We have no official connection to Reddit Inc.

events.description:
A Summer Night in Songs - Asian Rainbow Chorus (ARC) Season 2 Summer Concert

This summer, ARC presents a program centered on resilience, identity, and shared hope.

Through music, we celebrate the strength to flourish even in difficult times. In the wake of the pandemic, amid experiences of discrimination shared by Asian communities, and facing a turbulent environment for immigrants, we choose to respond through voice rather than silence.

From self-affirmation in I Am What I Am,
to cultural memory in Arirang and Hanyangmen Garden,
to poetic tenderness in Dirait-on,
and culminating in the holiness of Total Praise and Amazing Grace,

the concert becomes a journey of remembrance, intercultural understanding, and collective uplift.

In summer light, we sing.
In adversity, we bloom.

Program

We are the Asian Rainbow Chorus (ARC), founded in New York in July 2024. ARC is an Asian LGBTQ+ choir that centers on Mandarin-speaking Asians and is dedicated to building connections among overseas communities.

The Asian Rainbow Chorus (ARC) is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, fiscally sponsored by SilkPride.

Through music, we unite as a community — using our voices to express queer identity, cultural diversity, and transnational solidarity. ARC is dedicated to creating an inclusive space for Mandarin-speaking queer Asians, where members from diverse regions and backgrounds can share their stories, emotions, and strength through song.

Singing is also a form of direct action. ARC believes in the power of collective voice and remains committed to advancing social justice through music.

Experience Korea in the heart of NYC — a lively Korean Culture Playground with performance, K-beauty, food, drinks, and live DJ sets.

MEARI NORI Vol.1 : Korean Culture Playground

The echo of Korea arrives in the heart of New York.

In Korea, there's a word — '놀이 (Nori)': coming together to enjoy a show, share food and drinks, and lose yourself in the moment. It's the liveliest way to experience Korean culture.

On July 12th, Meari — a Korean Culture Collective — brings that spirit to the heart of Manhattan.

From the deep, haunting tones of the daegeum (Korean bamboo flute) to the elegant movement of Korean contemporary dance, the bold procession of a traditional royal marching band, and a live DJ to heat up the night — the stage flows without pause, between tradition and modernity, while the flavors of Korean food and drink keep pouring.

Off to one side, a K-Beauty pop-up brings you face-to-face with a curated lineup of cosmetic brands straight from Korea.

Tradition and modernity, the joy of watching and the joy of tasting — all echoing in one place. This is Meari Nori, the Korean Culture Playground. Come play with us!

Join The Libi Project to celebrate the launch of The Libi Journal @thelibjournal and to celebrate our 3rd birthday!

An extension of The Libi Project community, The Libi Journal documents the stories of mission-driven individuals working at the intersection of food, culture, and entrepreneurship — the very people who have built the Libi community over the past two years.

Pre-order your copy here!

The Libi Journal features:

Jess Eng @goudatalks
Behzad Jamshidi @behzj
Thu Pham Buser @thubuser
CarlaFinley @apt2bread
Kiyomi Tanigawa @brooklyn_misomaru

To celebrate, we'll have some delicious bites for purchase by Chef Mei Liao x Day With Mei, wine from Hudson Wilder, and small-batch artisanal ice cream from Little Rat Ice Cream, along with complimentary drinks by NON, a chef-crafted range of non-alcoholic beverages.

Meijie “Mei” Liao @daywithmei is a James Beard Foundation and IACP–nominated recipe developer and content creator based in Brooklyn, New York. Born to Sichuanese parents and raised in California, she is the creator of @daywithmei, where she shares approachable recipes that work to dispel food stigmas. Her latest work explores the everyday rituals and values of the Sichuanese diaspora, from stewarding century-old pickles to championing offal.

Charles & Susan Raben started Little Rat Ice Cream @littlerat.icecream in the summer of 2025, out of their kitchen in Ridgewood, Queens. Little Rat is a small-batch artisanal ice cream brand.

NON is a chef-led, non-alcoholic beverage company redefining the drinking experience with bold, uncompromising flavour. Born out of the fine dining scene and crafted in Melbourne, Australia, NON creates wine alternatives that are made from real produce, infused with culinary thinking, and built to pair with food. They don’t mimic wine—NON makes something entirely new. Each NON variant is carefully formulated with ingredients like whole fruits, herbs, spices, teas, and verjus to deliver a complex, layered drinking experience without alcohol, preservatives, or artificial flavours.

Budds will walk us through a curated selection of essays, articles, and writings, and then a group writing workshop will explore the themes of missing stories and voices in our understanding of the city today.

Attendees will learn how to look more closely at the cityscape, frame distinct perspectives on place and space, and share impactful narratives. The session is grounded in the urban context of Gowanus, a neighborhood that has witnessed tremendous physical and cultural change in recent years, and will include a brief walking tour.

This workshop will culminate in a zine of collected writings by the group. All ages and levels of writing experience are welcomed and encouraged.

6 pm — Doors open, reading session beings
6:30 pm — Group discussion and reflection, led and guided by Diana Budds
7 pm — Personal writing time by the Gowanus Canal
7:45 pm — Regroup at Van Alen; option to share work aloud

This event is presented by the Van Alen Vanguard, a dynamic network of emerging design leaders. Diana Budds is among the inaugural cohort developing public programs and exhibitions that reimagine designers’ roles in civic life and center equity, resilience, and local knowledge.

events.title: Paradise Aquarium
events.description:
An evening of short films curated by Emily May Jampel featuring works from four emerging New York-based filmmakers depicting themes of water, city life, and the search for connection. Presented in collaboration with NowHere.

This program of short films features works by Eve Liu, Jeannie Sui Wonders, Alexa Lim Haas, and Kevin Xian Ming Yu. Following a Chinese manicurist in Miami, a nonbinary Asian-American from Queens attempting to reconnect with their estranged father, a middle school girl at a sleepover in suburban Michigan, and two unhinged female filmmakers in Chinatown Manhattan who decide to break up with their boyfriends and make a bold decision with their lives, these films capture dynamic individuals across the Asian diaspora navigating new environments, family tensions, isolation and fervid rivalry.

These award-winning films have collectively screened at festivals worldwide, including Cannes Directors' Fortnight, Venice, Sundance, SXSW, New York Film Festival, Palm Springs ShortFest, Aspen Shortsfest, and more. These four directors are currently developing their first feature-length projects and represent some of the most exciting new voices in independent film today.

This program is co-produced and curated by Emily May Jampel, presented in partnership with NowHere.

Program

7:00pm - Doors
7:30pm - Screening begins promptly
8:15pm - Q&A with filmmakers in attendance moderated by Emily May Jampel
9:00pm - Finish

Films:

Moon Lake dir. Jeannie Sui Wonders
Agua Viva dir. Alexa Lim Haas
Yú Cì (Fish Bones) dir. Kevin Xian Ming Yu
Nervous Energy dir. Eve Liu

Curator

Emily May Jampel is a director, writer, and actress from Honolulu based in Brooklyn. Her films have screened at festivals including Palm Springs, Aspen ShortsFest, and Hawaiʻi International Film Festival. She previously worked as Development Executive at The Department of Motion Pictures and served as creative consultant on Constance Tsang's feature Blue Sun Palace, which premiered at Cannes Critics’ Week 2024 and received four Independent Spirit Award nominations. Emily is a 2026 NYSCA grantee and 2024–2025 UFO Short Film Lab fellow hosted at BAM, and was listed on the 2024 Dazed100.

Filmmakers

Eve Liu is a Chinese Australian filmmaker based in New York City. She holds an MFA from NYU Tisch, where her thesis film, Nervous Energy, was executive produced by Spike Lee and screened at Cannes, SXSW, and NYFF, among others.

Jeannie Sui Wonders is a filmmaker based in New York City. She began her career with Roger Corman and Sofia Coppola, and studied filmmaking at Harvard. Her debut fiction short film, MOON LAKE, premiered in competition at the 2024 81st Venice Film Festival.

Alexa Lim Haas (she/they) is a Filipino-American artist, filmmaker, and bodyworker from NYC. Their interdisciplinary practice moves between film and somatics, exploring liminal femmes, tropical futurism, and nonverbal communication. They are best known for their short film Agua Viva (Sundance, Rotterdam, SXSW), following an immigrant manicurist navigating mistranslation and intimacy.

Kevin Xian Ming Yu (they/them) is a non-binary filmmaker from Queens, NY. Kevin received the 2025 NewFest/Netflix New Voices Filmmaker Grant and they were a 2024 Film at Lincoln Center Artist Academy Fellow. Their latest short film ”i saw you in the flood”, premiered at SXSW 2026 and their previous short “Yú Cì (Fish Bones),” premiered at SXSW 2025. Both films were supported by the UFO Short Film Lab. Kevin is committed to telling stories that start empathetic conversations across generations within Asian-American communities and diaspora.

events.title: Critical Thinking Club - Is therapy making us more resilient, or more reactive?
events.description:
New Yorkers argue a lot. Disagreement has the power to bring us closer together, but most of us never learned how. Somewhere along the way, speed replaced clarity, and certainty replaced curiosity. The result: we're more polarized, and less understanding.

Critical Thinking Club aims to reverse that trend by providing space for busy New Yorkers who want to think more clearly and communicate more thoughtfully. The goal isn’t to "win" an argument, it’s to improve how we listen, disagree, and express our opinions without distancing each other.

Our next theme is, Is therapy making us more resilient, or more reactive?

We’ll use this topic to practice critical thinking skills like…

Articulating views you disagree with

Separating personal experience from broader evidence

Noticing hidden assumptions, in yourself and others

Revising your position without compromising your values or credibility

What to expect

2 comedians debate and a philosophy professor moderates

Small-group conversations with guided prompts

A psychologically safe environment for disagreement

The evening begins with a moderated discussion between 2 comedians who take opposite sides. A professor of philosophy moderates and steps in when reasoning breaks down, or a common fallacy appears. The moderator pauses to explain, and suggests how reasoning could be improved. Mistakes are expected and useful. The goal is for all of us to share the learnings. Then, everyone practices in small groups.

Agenda

6:30  - 7 PM - Arrive, chat, find a seat

7 - 7:05 PM - Summarize what to expect, introduce the moderator and speakers

7:05 - 7:35 PM - Opening statements, Q&A, and closing statements

7:35 - 8:05 - Small group discussions (4-6 people) to practice sound critical thinking skills with the help of guided prompts

8:05 - 8:10 - Wrap-up, participants can share key takeaways from their group discussions

8:10 - 8:45ish - Socialize with the speakers and participants

9 - after party Rocka Rolla

Speakers

We’re excited to have Greg Nirshberg moderating our next discussion. Greg is the Professor of Philosophy at Iona University, an advisor for the university’s interdisciplinary neuroscience program, and he received his Ph.D. in philosophy at the University of Wisconsin-Madison in 2023, https://www.iona.edu/faculty/nirshberg-gregory

Our next speakers will be standup extraordinaire Natalie Lin @natalielin._, and Comedy Cellar regular, Bassam Shawl @bassamshawl.

If you want to understand others better, and be better understood, join us!

events.title: Soft Speakers: Hands on eTextiles Workshop
events.description:
A hands-on exploration of sound and textiles, where you'll design and create soft speakers by weaving with conductive threads.

Soft Speakers: Hands on eTextiles Workshop is an introduction to the world of eTextiles, where electronics and textiles merge. In this 3-hour session, you’ll design and build your own woven soft speaker using conductive thread and magnets. Designed as an introductory workshop, participants will come away with the basics of weaving and the fundamentals of crafting sound-emitting textiles.

This workshop is perfect for creatives, technologists, craftspeople, and anyone curious about the intersection of electronics and materials. You’ll leave not only with a working fabric speaker prototype but also with new insights into how textiles can be used as a medium for technology. By the end of the session, you’ll be equipped with practical skills to experiment further with eTextiles in your own work.

A hand loom and all necessary materials are included in the registration fee.

Program

Welcome & Introduction: Overview of the workshop and materials

Tool Demo: Weaving techniques and speaker basics

Brief Lecture on Speaker Theory: How sound works and the role of materials in speaker design

Discussion of Relevant Precedents: Exploration of eTextile projects and inspirations

Crafting Time: Design and create your own woven speaker using conductive fabrics and threads

Symphony of Speakers: Test and showcase everyone’s finished speakers in a collective sound experience

Wrap-Up & Next Steps: Final thoughts and ideas for future experimentation

Learning Outcomes

How to use a tapestry loom

How to weave double cloth

How to create a soft speaker

The basics of speaker engineering

The basic of eTextiles

Resources

Take a look at this workshop page.

Facilitators

Craftwork is a multidisciplinary design and art studio exploring the nature of textiles and technology through installations, storytelling, and material-based research. Craftwork co-founders, Nicole Yi Messier and Victoria Manganiello will be teaching this workshop.

events.title: Club Rest Stop: Social Rest
events.description:
Club Rest Stop × Centr: Social Rest

The time to rest is now. Join us July 19th — same time, same place.

A collaboration between Club Rest Stop and Centr.

Dear Resties,

Our first meeting was such a hit that we're coming back next month! As you know, Club Rest Stop is all about learning and practicing the seven types of rest. Last time, we explored the rest types and practiced physical rest together. This month, we're turning to social rest — learning how to gauge our social batteries, set boundaries around social engagement, and, of course, rest together.

🌟 In collaboration with Centr

This month, we've teamed up with Centr to bring social rest to life. Centr, founded by Mahika Hari, is a free app that helps you be a better villager by maintaining and deepening the relationships you already have: remembering the personal details that matter, reconnecting more intentionally, and understanding your own social battery.

Centr's work lives right at the intersection of social connection, community, and wellness, exactly what social rest is about. Mahika will co-facilitate with a short talk on the basics, followed by activities built for practicing different forms of social rest in groups.

🧺 What to expect

Rest kits (while supplies last!)

A brief, friendly talk on social rest

A game station for easy, low-key connection

A book reading station to slow down

A quiet zone for resting on your own terms

And more!

📍 The details

🗓️ Sunday, July 19 · ⏰ 2–4 PM (programming begins at 2:30 with the social-rest presentation, followed by rest activities and quiet time) · 📍 The Great Hill, Central Park, NYC

Getting there: Take the B/C uptown to 103rd St — the exit is directly across from a Central Park entrance. It's a ~5-minute walk (use Google Maps) to the Great Hill, located right across from the bathrooms (if you're facing the bathrooms, the Great Hill is on your right). You'll find us set up on a rainbow blanket. Alternatively, enter at 106th and follow the signs for "Great Hill."

🎒 What to bring

Mostly, just yourself. If you'd like, feel free to bring something that can be shared: craft materials, cards, snacks, baked goods, etc. It's never required, but it's a lovely way to break the ice with your fellow Resties.

💌 A few extras

The community we're building is so special, and I can't wait to rest with you again.

New here? Everything you need is in your welcome packet, and we post updates on our TikTok.

Curious about Centr? Sign up for early access to the beta and join the waitlist at centrtheapp.com — follow along on TikTok and Instagram, and/or follow the Centr Luma page.

Mahika is also offering Resties a discounted rate to her Wellness Wednesday community event on sleep health — use code RESTIES and join us in learning about how to actually combat burnout and fix your energy levels while enjoying dinner bites, drinks, goody bags, and making your own embroidered sleep mask!

Questions? Reach us anytime at cthpress@gmail.com.

It's time to take a break. See you soon. 🤍

events.description:
The BKLYN MILE, presented by HOKA will take place on Sunday August 2, 2026 

The mile is an iconic distance. Unique in its ability to stoke the fire of the most competitive runner, but also in its wide appeal to those of all abilities and experience levels.

Participants in Brooklyn’s iconic road mile run along Kent Avenue through the heart of Williamsburg. The course is fun, fast and exhilarating. Personal bests abound for experienced runners, and a unique opportunity for beginners and/or back-of-the-packers to experience a unique trip along Williamsburg’s main artery.

REGISTRATION TO OPEN AT NOON ON FRIDAY, JUNE 12, 2026

Updates are coming. Stay ready.

events.title: *FREE EVENT* Slow Summer at Brooklyn Bridge Park
events.description:
Grab a blanket and a book and meet us on the Pier 6 lawn for the kind of Sunday afternoon summer was made for.

Reading Rhythms is partnering with Brooklyn Bridge Park for Slow Summer. This is the second of three Slow Summer reading parties happening at BBP this season. Live music. Waterfront views. A couple hundred people reading together for the afternoon.

What to expect:

A low-key, unplugged Sunday afternoon on the waterfront

The Reading Rhythms experience, but outside, including 1 hour of reading to live music

1-on-1 breakout conversations and group discussion with fellow readers

What to bring:

BYO blanket (or towel, or yoga mat, no chairs on the lawn, please!)

BYO book (any book, any genre, whatever you want to read)

BYO sunscreen, water, sunglasses, and hat

A friend if you want, or come solo!

This is free and open to the public, but RSVPs help us plan, so claim your spot.

Want to plan ahead? Slow Summer returns to Brooklyn Bridge Park on September 20.

Reading Rhythms is a series of welcoming reading parties designed to make it easy for people to gather and read socially. We invite you to bring any book you want and facilitate silent reading periods, as well as 1-on-1 breakouts, and group discussions to pull on the threads of the books that everyone is engaging with.

In a world that can often feel devoid of interest and meaningful connection, we bring readers together to reconnect with our love of learning, to experience the wonder of beautiful venues and curated music, and to gather amongst all of the people who want to be in that very same room.

Our favorite conversations happen in the Reading Rhythms mobile app. Join us there → https://readingrhythms.app
___________________________________________________________________

Disclaimer: By purchasing a ticket, you agree to have your photo/video used for social media/marketing materials, both as property of Reading Rhythms and other publications that may be present at our reading parties. Additionally, your purchase is final and no refunds will be given under any circumstances. By completing your purchase, you will also be automatically added to our newsletter to receive updates and event information."
